beats.narration: Fatigue is the king of service failures — cracking from repeated stress cycles below yield strength, growing a little with every cycle.

payload.title: FATIGUE CRACK
payload.sub: The king of service failures, one stress cycle at a time.

beats.narration: Every fatigue crack starts at a stress riser. Keyways, fillets, thread roots, even old corrosion pits — wherever stress concentrates, cracks begin.
The fracture face is a logbook: the origin at the riser, beach marks rippling outward, and the rough final zone where it tore.

payload.heading: Read the fracture face like a logbook
payload.note: BEACH MARKS CURVE AROUND THE ORIGIN

beats.narration: On the surface it's a tight hairline with no deformation, crossing machining marks. Scratches follow the tool path — cracks follow the stress.
So when a part breaks, remember: ripples point home. Trace the beach marks backward and they lead you straight to the origin.

payload.sub: Trace the beach marks back to the origin.
